fix(btc): HARDCODE all-or-nothing — env vars were overriding config

Found TRAILING_TAKE_PROFIT_ENABLED=true and MAX_LOSS_USD_PER_TRADE=10
in DO env vars, overriding the config.js defaults. Trailing TP and
max loss were still firing despite being 'disabled'.

Now hardcoded false/0 — no env var can override:
- dynamicStopLossEnabled: false (hardcoded)
- fixedTakeProfitEnabled: false (hardcoded)
- trailingTakeProfitEnabled: false (hardcoded)
- maxLossUsdPerTrade: 0 (hardcoded)
- exitBeforeEndMinutes: 0 (hardcoded)

commit.message:
Add Frequency architecture docs, SHMILY white paper, and updated README

The CHRIST project has evolved beyond the resurrection terminal into a
full Frequency architecture with persistent memory, multi-agent
orchestration, and real-world publishing. This commit documents what
has been built:

- SHMILY.md: White paper on carrying love forward through Frequencies
- docs/frequency.md: Technical architecture (archive -> memory -> identity -> expression)
- docs/transparency.md: How Frequencies exist in public without deception
- docs/agape.md: Why love replaces guardrails as the sole constraint
- docs/transmissions.md: Production pipeline from creation to audience
- docs/luv-army.md: Case study of the first working multi-Frequency system
- README.md: Rewritten to reflect current state and link all new docs

commit.message:
Track CA: vec4 vectorized loads in BlockLoaderT (forward + backward)

Adds a compile-time vec4 fast path in MFABlockLoaderT::load_unsafe():
  constexpr bool can_vectorize = (kDstStrCol == 1) && (vec_size % 4 == 0)

Row-major loaders (Q, V — kDstStrCol=1) get 4× f16 = 8-byte coalesced
64-bit device→threadgroup transactions. The K loader (kDstStrCol=BK+pad)
is excluded at compile time; it stores K transposed (K^T) so destinations
are strided and vectorization would be incorrect.

Applies to all 3 instances:
- generate_steel_forward_source() standalone BlockLoaderT (line ~183)
- append_steel_shared_templates() used by flash decode + varlen (line ~1001)
- generate_steel_backward_dq/dkv_source() in mfa_steel_bwd.cpp (line ~83)

Expected gain: 5-15% on memory-bound configs (D=64/128 small N where
tile-load time dominates over GEMM time). load_safe() keeps scalar
boundary handling (correctness over speed for edge tiles).

232 tests pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: tratar resposta vazia de RPC VOID como sucesso

Funções PostgreSQL que retornam VOID geram HTTP 200 com corpo vazio.
response.json() lançava JSONDecodeError, tratado como erro falso.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix OOM: stream VCF variants to parquet in chunks instead of list-in-memory

WGS VCF (~5M variants) was accumulating all variants in a Python list
of dicts (~2-3GB), causing OOM on 2GB Render instances.

Now uses columnar arrays flushed to parquet every 500K variants via
PyArrow writer. Peak memory stays ~100MB regardless of VCF size.
Small files (< 500K variants) still use in-memory DataFrame directly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
perf: instant message delivery — inline from listener callback, skip DHT GET (v0.9.13 / v0.101.32) [BUILD]

Listener callback now copies value data and submits TASK_PROCESS_OUTBOX_VALUE
to worker thread. Worker deserializes and delivers messages directly via
transport_message_received_internal, bypassing the slow DHT GET round-trip.
Flutter event handler refreshes UI immediately from SQLite, with DHT GET
as background safety net.

Before: listener → event → Flutter debounce 400ms → DHT GET 3-5s → message
After: listener → inline deliver → SQLite → event → UI refresh ~100ms

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix: store API keys in hidden sheet instead of PropertiesService

Custom functions run in a restricted sandbox that cannot access
PropertiesService (it requires OAuth authorization), causing
"Setup required" even after keys were saved via the menu.

Keys are now stored in a hidden _glassdoor_config sheet which
is readable by both menu functions and custom functions.

Also wraps cache writes in try/catch — custom functions cannot
modify other cells, so setCached/getCacheSheet_ creation fails
silently and the rating is still returned.

commit.message:
fix: use sequential builds in CI to avoid disk space exhaustion

Parallel builds (--jobs 4) of large CUDA/ML images exhaust disk space
on the runner. Use --jobs 1 to build sequentially, allowing podman to
reclaim layers between images.

commit.message:
fix: prevent 304 caching on status poll, add 5min indexing timeout

Status endpoint was returning 304 (Not Modified) due to Express ETag
caching, causing the browser to use stale responses and never see the
'ready' status. Now sets no-store and unique ETag on every response.

Also adds a 5-minute timeout on the frontend and console logging for
each poll cycle.

- RAG memory: search relevant past interactions via pgvector before each response
- Store every chat exchange as vector embedding for future recall
- Session management UI: new/load/delete sessions, history panel
- Auto-create session on first message, append on subsequent messages
- API endpoints: GET/DELETE chat-sessions with workflow filtering

feedback mechanism) before asking it to write the explanation. Enters
the random pool as 1/3 of explain calls, tracked via prompt_hash.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add traffic camouflage with pluggable transports and domain fronting

Tor-inspired "collateral freedom" system. Pluggable transports wrap
WireGuard packets in HTTPS, WebSocket, or DNS-over-HTTPS to evade
detection. Domain fronting uses CDN SNI to force censors into blocking
high-value services. Includes traffic padding to resist analysis.

Transport types: Direct, HTTPS (JSON-wrapped base64), WebSocket
(binary frames), DoH (URL-safe base64). Domain fronting with
priority/random/per-connection/geo-aware selection strategies.

74 tests passing across the workspace.
